author: Dr. Marcus Hale (Hepatology)
content:
HEPATOLOGY CONTINUITY NOTE — UNIVERSITY
Date: 11/5/2026

INTERVAL HISTORY: 49F (now 50) with decompensated alcohol-related cirrhosis. Recurrent ascites and a right hepatic hydrothorax cause episodic dyspnea and abdominal distension. Hepatic encephalopathy controlled on lactulose (titrated to 3 BMs/day) and rifaximin. She has been SOBER for ~6 months at this visit, engaged in an outpatient recovery program and AA. Lives in a rural county ~90 minutes away; her sister drives her to appointments — distance is a recurring barrier to frequent visits/procedures.

EXAM: stable; distended abdomen with shifting dullness; decreased right basilar breath sounds. No asterixis today.

ASSESSMENT/PLAN:
- Decompensated cirrhosis with recurrent ascites + right hydrothorax; on spironolactone 50 / furosemide 20.
- Continue lactulose/rifaximin for HE.
- Begin formal liver-transplant evaluation given decompensation and progress toward the sobriety milestone.
- Counsel on sodium restriction; reinforce abstinence and recovery engagement.
- Anticipate that fluid control may become difficult; will escalate diuretics cautiously and consider mechanical drainage if refractory.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Marcus Hale, MD (Hepatology). 11/5/2026.
